high: requires more wave power or paddling precision

rules: Rocker must be interpreted with outline, rail and bottom contour.
Low rocker is not automatically beginner-friendly if the board is short, narrow or low volume.
High rocker performance boards generally demand stronger technique and waves.

rails.boxy_full: stable, buoyant and forgiving but less sensitive
rails.low_refined: sensitive, engaged and controlled but less forgiving
rails.pinched: precise engagement and hold; technically demanding

bottom_contours.flat: speed and planing with a direct feel
bottom_contours.single_concave: lift, speed and channelled flow; can feel powerful on rail
bottom_contours.double_concave: adds transition and looseness through the rear half
bottom_contours.vee: helps rail-to-rail transition and control, often through tail
bottom_contours.channels: additional drive and hold with a more committed line
bottom_contours.hull_or_convex: flowing trim and smooth rail transitions; specialised feel
